title: cracca adunca
intro: Cracca adunca, also known as the Sticky Cracca or Western Sticky Cracca, is a perennial legume native to western North America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating diarrhea, dysentery, and other gastrointestinal issues due to its anthelmintic and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, alleviating symptoms of respiratory issues, and supporting digestive health. Cracca adunca can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, to harness its medicinal properties.
